summary: Fabric v2.0 rc.1 — Clean rebrand foundation. Establish v2.0 directory layout (.fabric/knowledge/{5 type subdirs}/ + pending/), dual-root home (~/.fabric/), v2.0 frontmatter schema (id/type/maturity/layer/layer_reason/created_at, all flat scalars), path-decoupled stable_id mechanism (KP-/KT- prefix + monotonic counter persisted in agents.meta.json), and init-time deterministic scan producing 4-7 baseline entries (tech stack/module structure/build/style/CI/README). Adapt existing 2 MCP tools and existing doctor checks to new schema. NO new MCP tools, NO archive/review/import skills, NO Stop hooks, NO doctor --lint, NO README/docs rewrite — those are rc.2/3/4 scope.
approach: 8-task breakdown grouped by deliverable: (1) delete legacy data, independent. (2) define v2.0 schema in shared/, foundation for rest. (3) implement stable_id counter + allocator on top of schema. (4) update frontmatter parser to extract new fields. (5) adapt existing MCP services (plan-context + rule-sections) to dual-root scan + new schema. (6) adapt existing doctor checks (rename + extend, no new lint checks). (7) implement deterministic init-scan command producing 4-7 baseline entries from forensic.json. (8) rewrite fabric init flow as 4-stage pipeline (bootstrap → scan → mcp → hooks) that creates v2.0 layout + invokes scan + ensures dual-root home. True dependency graph: 1 independent; 2 blocks 3,4,6,7,8; 3 blocks 7,8; 4 blocks 5; 7 blocks 8. Tasks 1,2 can start in parallel; 4,6 can run in parallel after 2; 5 follows 4; 7 follows 2+3; 8 is the convergence (depends on 1,2,3,7).
estimated_time: 550 minutes (~9 hours focused) ≈ 1.5 weeks calendar with reviews/integration

design_decisions.decision: Schema (TASK-002) is foundation; parser/counter/MCP/doctor/scan all consume it
design_decisions.rationale: Schema defines the contract; behavior layers (parser, persistence, MCP, doctor, scan) cannot encode/decode fields they don't know about. Establishing the schema first prevents schema-drift across implementation tasks.
design_decisions.tradeoff: TASK-002 becomes a critical path bottleneck; mitigated by keeping it pure type/schema work (no behavior) so it lands quickly and unblocks 4-way parallelism downstream.

design_decisions.decision: Frontmatter stays flat scalars; no upgrade to gray-matter / YAML parser
design_decisions.rationale: Existing regex parser at rule-meta-builder.ts:748-785 is constrained to flat scalars + flow arrays. v2.0 fields (id/type/maturity/layer/layer_reason/created_at) all fit this constraint. Parser upgrade is out-of-scope refactor.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Cannot represent nested metadata like `maturity: {level, last_review}`; mitigated by using flat sibling fields (created_at, layer_reason) instead.

design_decisions.decision: stable_id counter lives in agents.meta.json `counters: {KP, KT}` envelope
design_decisions.rationale: Reuses existing durable single-source-of-truth artifact; no new files/sync points. Counter envelope is additive (optional in schema) so v1.x meta files load without migration error.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Single point of failure if meta corrupts; mitigated by counter_desync detection added to doctor stable_id_collision check (TASK-006).

design_decisions.decision: Dual-root scanning: repo .fabric/knowledge/ AND ~/.fabric/knowledge/ merged at request time
design_decisions.rationale: gitignore cannot filter by frontmatter; personal entries must be physically separate from team entries to avoid accidental git commits. Single root with frontmatter-based filter was rejected for this reason.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Adds I/O per request (two trees scanned); mitigated by 5-minute selection_token cache that reuses parsed nodes within request lifecycle.

design_decisions.decision: Default layer for init-scan baseline entries is 'team' (KT-)
design_decisions.rationale: Project artifacts (tech stack, module structure, build config, CI) are inherently team-level — they describe the repo, not personal preferences. Matches rc.4 fabric-import skill default-layer decision.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Counter KT advances during init; counter KP stays at 0 until first personal write (rc.2 archive flow). Acceptable — counters are independent.

design_decisions.decision: Doctor adapts existing 4 v1.x-coupled checks but does NOT add new lint checks
design_decisions.rationale: rc.4 introduces the 6 new lint checks (orphan demote / stale archive / id duplicate / layer mismatch / index drift / pending overdue) which depend on event types defined by rc.2/3. rc.1 just keeps doctor green on the new layout.
design_decisions.tradeoff: No safety net for type/maturity/layer correctness until rc.4; mitigated by parser-level validation in TASK-004 that warns on malformed frontmatter.

design_decisions.decision: Init flow becomes 4-stage pipeline with scan inserted between bootstrap and mcp
design_decisions.rationale: Scan needs the v2.0 directory layout and agents.meta.json envelope (created in bootstrap stage); MCP and hooks stages need agents.meta.json populated (output of scan). This ordering is forced by data dependencies.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Init takes ~500ms longer; user gets non-empty knowledge base in exchange. Per handoff acceptance #6, non-empty post-init base is an explicit goal.

design_decisions.decision: Delete legacy artifacts immediately (TASK-001) instead of moving to .fabric-v1-archive/
design_decisions.rationale: User explicit clean-slate preference (recorded in long-term memory and handoff decision_context). No current users → migration tax is zero → archival is dead weight.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Deletion is irrecoverable outside git; mitigated by single-commit batching that makes revert trivial.

data_flow.diagram: user → fab init → [stage 1: bootstrap creates v2.0 layout + agents.meta.json with empty counters] → [stage 2: fab scan reads forensic.json → builds 4-7 entries → KnowledgeIdAllocator → writes .fabric/knowledge/{models|guidelines|processes}/*.md + updates agents.meta.json counters/nodes + emits init_scan_completed event] → [stage 3: mcp config write] → [stage 4: hooks config write] → repo ready. Runtime: client → MCP → fab_plan_context (dual-root scan {.fabric/knowledge/, ~/.fabric/knowledge/} → merge → filter deprecated → return description_index with layer tags + selection_token) → fab_get_rule_sections (consume token, return 4 sections per rule).

stages.output: v2.0 ruleDescriptionSchema + agentsMetaSchema with counters envelope + KnowledgeType/Maturity/Layer enums

stages.input: Schema + new node with knowledge metadata
stages.output: agents.meta.json with declared stable_id (KP-/KT- format) + counter monotonicity invariant
transformations: atomic write of meta with counters

stages.output: Parsed RuleDescription with all 6 new fields populated
Merged description_index with team + personal entries
Fully scaffolded v2.0 .fabric/ + ~/.fabric/ + 4-7 baseline entries + clean doctor + working MCP

dependencies: packages/shared schemas exported to packages/server and packages/cli
fab_plan_context selection_token TTL machinery (existing 5-min in-memory map)
forensic.json from existing forensic-builder (init stage 1 output)

out_of_scope: Any LLM-driven extraction or review logic (rc.2/3/4)

quality_check.method: manual self-review against task validation checklist + acceptance_criteria_mapping coverage
